evidence: HttpApiUtils.getCorsAllowOrigin() reads System.getProperty at call time, not at class load — the Javadoc explicitly notes this was moved from a static final field to a method to avoid the stale-snapshot problem; this is a correct design decision
BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor performs I/O (BlackLab.open) — declared throws IOException making the side effect explicit; no hidden I/O at class load time
WordSketchApiServer constructor builds handler objects but defers port binding to start() — a comment explicitly documents this separation of concerns
All static final regex patterns in BlackLabSnippetParser are compiled at class load time — safe, immutable, no ordering dependency
RelationUtils.RELATION_ALIASES is a static Map.of(...) at class load — immutable, no dependency on other modules at load time

SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator both have deprecated no-GrammarConfig constructors that fall back to hardcoded pattern constants — these create a parallel initialization code path but are documented as deprecated-for-removal; production use always passes GrammarConfig
GrammarConfigLoader.load() is called explicitly from Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand, not at class initialization time — no hidden import-time I/O
ExplorationHandlers constructor validates grammarConfig != null at construction time with IllegalArgumentException — fails fast rather than deferring to first handler call

No circular init dependencies found: the dependency graph is WordSketchApiServer → handlers → SemanticFieldExplorer / QueryExecutor → CollocateQueryHelper → BlackLabIndex, which is a clean acyclic chain

strengths: Port binding explicitly deferred to start() in WordSketchApiServer with a documenting comment — construction and network binding are cleanly separated
All I/O (index open, config file load) occurs at explicit call sites in Main, not at class or static-block initialization time
HttpApiUtils CORS value correctly read at call time rather than class load, preventing the stale-snapshot problem
All dependencies are passed via constructor injection (executor, grammarConfig, semanticFieldExplorer) — no hidden singleton lookups or static service locators
ExplorationHandlers validates its required grammarConfig argument at construction time, failing fast rather than at first request
initialization_coupling.issue_character: The codebase has minimal initialization coupling issues; the main observation is the deprecated fallback constructors in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator that maintain a parallel hardcoded-pattern code path.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: Java's class initialization model and this codebase's explicit dependency injection approach mean there are essentially no import-time side effects or boot-order surprises. The one pattern worth noting — deprecated fallback constructors with hardcoded patterns — is properly marked and documented. Against an 80-anchor of 'solid but uneven', this codebase scores above that: initialization is clean throughout, with the deferred-start pattern in the server being especially well-handled. The deprecated constructors are the only residual coupling concern, holding the score back from 90+.

issues.summary: Deprecated no-config constructors in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator maintain a hardcoded-pattern fallback code path that bypasses GrammarConfig
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor) deprecated constructor calls this(executor, null), triggering deriveNounCqlConstraint(null) which returns FALLBACK_NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT = "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]"
CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor) deprecated constructor similarly falls back to FALLBACK_ADJECTIVE_PATTERN = "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]"
Tests using StubExecutor pass the deprecated constructor path — any test changes to the fallback pattern could silently diverge from the config-driven path
issues.suggestion: Accelerate removal of the deprecated constructors: update all remaining test callers to use the GrammarConfig-accepting constructors (supply a test grammar config via GrammarConfigHelper.requireTestConfig()), then delete the deprecated overloads and the FALLBACK_* constants

root_causes: Test isolation: deprecated constructors were retained primarily to support tests that don't want to supply a grammar config; unifying test infrastructure would eliminate the need
